spec_match.rationale: The page now maps directly to the spec's safe and unsafe insertion contract: setHTML(html, options), appendHTML(html, options), setHTMLUnsafe(html, options), sanitizer options, and runScripts gating.

interactive.rationale: Payload presets, editable HTML, sanitizer selection, append count, unsafe sanitizer, and runScripts toggles all drive live rendered output, DOM serialization, and hazard counts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still demonstrates the feature-index promise of comparing setHTML(), appendHTML(), setHTMLUnsafe(), and innerHTML through sanitized and unsanitized paths.

edge_cases.rationale: The presets and controls cover script elements, event attributes, javascript: URLs, embeds/forms, declarative shadow DOM markup, DOM clobbering attributes, fallback behavior, and unsafe-only runScripts.

references.rationale: The references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5054329641893888.

design_system.rationale: The page uses project CSS variables for colors, overrides button background and color together, remains interactive rather than card-only, and keeps fallback messaging visible.

summary: The sanitizer concept has been rebuilt into a live API lab with explicit native feature detection, exact method call readouts, sanitizer option controls, append repetition, unsafe option toggles, live DOM dumps, and the required ChromeStatus reference. The previous major reference finding is cleared.
